modules.name: Programming Concepts

projects.title: Semester Planner and Folder Organizer

modules.name: Computer Systems Architecture
modules.description: CPU architecture, memory hierarchy, storage systems. Build system profilers and hardware monitors.

projects.title: System Hardware Profiler
projects.description: Collect CPU, memory, and disk information from the machine and print a hardware summary.
explanation: The script gathers system-level details using psutil.
It reports the processor, core count, memory size, and disk usage.
This is useful for hardware reviews and troubleshooting.

modules.name: Computer Network Engineering
modules.description: OSI model, TCP/IP stack, IP addressing, subnetting. Build network discovery and diagnostic tools.

projects.title: Network Host Discovery Tool
projects.description: Scan a local network range and list hosts that respond to a TCP port probe.
projects.code:
import ipaddress
import socket


def find_live_hosts(network, port=22, timeout=0.2):
    net = ipaddress.ip_network(network, strict=False)
    results = []
    for ip in net.hosts():
        sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
        sock.settimeout(timeout)
        try:
            sock.connect((str(ip), port))
            results.append(str(ip))
        except OSError:
            pass
        finally:
            sock.close()
    return results


if __name__ == '__main__':
    hosts = find_live_hosts('192.168.1.0/24')
    print('Live hosts:', hosts)
explanation: The script iterates over a subnet and tries to connect to a specific TCP port.
A successful connection indicates that the host is active.
This is a practical first step in network discovery.

modules.name: Information Systems
modules.description: Information systems analysis, design methodologies, UML modeling. Build system design tools.

projects.title: Department Operations Reporter
projects.description: Summarize department workloads and export a simple operations report.
projects.code:
import csv


def write_report(records, output_path):
    fields = ['department', 'tasks', 'completed']
    with open(output_path, 'w', newline='', encoding='utf-8') as handle:
        writer = csv.DictWriter(handle, fieldnames=fields)
        writer.writeheader()
        writer.writerows(records)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    records = [
        {'department': 'IT', 'tasks': 15, 'completed': 12},
        {'department': 'Admin', 'tasks': 9, 'completed': 8},
    ]
    write_report(records, 'operations.csv')
    print('Report saved to operations.csv')
explanation: The program writes a CSV report from department operation data.
It formats records in a way that is useful for daily reporting or management reviews.
This reflects how information systems support decision-making.

modules.name: Natural Language Processing
modules.description: Text processing, sentiment analysis, named entity recognition. Process language with Python.

projects.title: Keyword Extractor
projects.description: Extract the most meaningful words from a text document using simple frequency analysis.
projects.code:
import re
from collections import Counter


def extract_keywords(text, limit=5):
    words = re.findall(r'\b[a-z]{3,}\b', text.lower())
    counter = Counter(words)
    return [word for word, _ in counter.most_common(limit)]


if __name__ == '__main__':
    text = 'Python is useful for text mining and natural language processing tasks.'
    print(extract_keywords(text))
explanation: The script tokenizes text and ranks words by frequency.
This is a lightweight approach to NLP that is easy to understand and build on.
It is a useful starting point for text analytics.
